tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine findings (cloudy, brown, ammoniacal) and abnormal bilirubin, glucose, and ketones indicate possible renal and metabolic disturbances. HbA1c is low, arguing against chronic hyperglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for possible acute or chronic kidney disease and metabolic derangements.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
